name: Ashley Evans
title: From Cutting Nets to the NBA: An examination of whether NCAA national championship experience translates to a more successful NBA career
bio: Ashley Evans is a senior at Arcadia University pursuing a degree in Sport Management with a minor in Law and Public Policy. Ashley is a 4-year member of the women's soccer team and has gained valuable industry experience through a variety of internships throughout her time at the university. Following her sophomore year, Ashley joined the Promotions Team for the Syracuse Mets, where she gained hands-on experience in guest services and game presentation while contributing to the overall fan experience. During her junior year, she completed a Business Operations Internship with the Philadelphia Wings, where she developed skills in community relations, marketing initiatives, and day-to-day business operations. Throughout her senior year, Ashley expanded her experience in sales and event management as a Group Sales Intern with the Philadelphia Flyers. Across these roles, she has developed strong professional skills in adaptability, leadership, and communication while building a well-rounded foundation in the sport management industry.

name: Taylor Gardner
title: What aspect of a golfer's game during the LPGA Tour influences their ranking and total prize money?
bio: Taylor Gardner is a senior at Arcadia University pursuing a major in Sport Management. Taylor is a four year athlete and a two year captain on the Arcadia Women's Golf team. Throughout her education she has indulged herself in several internships, gaining experience for the sports industry after college. Junior year Taylor worked in community engagement and marketing for the Delaware Blue Coats, the G-League affiliate to the Philadelphia 76ers. Following this internship she gained more personal communication skills in her client relations role with MSS Inc. Senior year she obtained a marketing and digital media internship with Special Olympics. These experiences has helped her develop communication skills, marketing techniques, and overall knowledge in the sports industry to enhance her skills for a career in sports after graduation.

name: Sage Goeke
title: Which statistics on the PGA Tour Lead to the Most Money?
bio: Sage Goeke is a senior at Arcadia pursuing a degree in Sport Management. Sage is a 4 year player on the Baseball team at Arcadia and has had 2 internships since being at Arcadia. He has worked with the New Jersey Marlins, a baseball organization out of New Jersey, and at the Northeast Baseball Facility. These experiences have helped him develop a love for working in sports, and helped him gain skills in communication and leadership which will help set him up for a future in the sport world.

name: Leah Hatcher
title: Are NCAA College Football Teams Becoming Less Competitive? An Analysis of Competitive Balance from 2000–2020
bio: Leah Hatcher is a senior at Arcadia University, majoring in Sport Management with a minor in Business Administration. She is a four-year dual-sport student-athlete, competing on the Women's Ice Hockey and Women's Tennis teams, and serves as captain of the women's ice hockey team. Originally from Rogers, Minnesota, Leah played high school hockey for the Rogers Royals before continuing her athletic and academic career at the collegiate level. In addition to her athletic commitments, Leah serves on the executive board as the MAC SAAC representative, representing Arcadia within the Middle Atlantic Conferences Student-Athlete Advisory Committee. She is also involved with Ed Snider Youth Hockey & Education, where she coaches and mentors youth athletes, helping them grow both on and off the ice. Through her experiences as a student-athlete, captain, and mentor, Leah has developed strong leadership, communication, and time-management skills. She plans to pursue a career in the sports industry where she can continue to make a positive impact through athletics.

name: Darius Isaac
title: Does Free-Throw Percentage Affect Winning in the NBA?
bio: Darius Isaac is a senior at Arcadia University pursuing a Bachelor of Arts Degree in Sport Management and a Master's Degree in Business Administration. Darius is a 4-year member of the Men's basketball team at Arcadia. After his sophomore season, he attended the 2024 NCAA Student-Athlete Leadership Forum in Bethesda, Maryland, from April 11th to 14th. With this, he was able to make connections with current employees of the NCAA. In the summer of 2024, Isaac worked as a Student Experience Leader at Arcadia. He worked closely with Admissions Staff and the Student Engagement staff to help acclimate first-year students to the University and its campus. These experiences helped build Isaac's emotional intelligence and ability to collaborate with multiple departments. Along with management skills and hard work this will help him pursue his career aspirations of working in professional sports.

name: Nas Johnson
title: What Factors Strongly Predict Winning in Division I Women's Basketball?
bio: Nas Johnson is a senior Sport Management major at Arcadia University and a fifth-year member of the Arcadia Men's Basketball team. During his collegiate career, Johnson has earned First Team All-MAC Freedom honors twice and became a 1,000-point scorer while helping lead the program to a MAC Freedom Championship. In addition to his playing career, Johnson has gained experience in basketball operations and player development through coaching youth camps, working with AAU programs, and serving as a team manager during his medical redshirt season. Through both academic and athletic experiences, he has developed strong leadership, communication, and teamwork skills. Following graduation, Johnson plans to pursue opportunities to play professional basketball while also exploring careers in basketball operations, coaching, or player development. He hopes to continue making an impact on the game by helping athletes grow both on and off the court.

name: Katerina Kiousis
title: The impact of Game Location on Goal Differential in the National Women's Soccer League
bio: Katerina Kiousis is a senior at Arcadia University pursuing a degree in Sport Management with a minor in Business Administration. She is a four-year member of Arcadia's Women's Soccer team and has gained a variety of internship experiences throughout her college career. Katerina has developed professional experience through internships and positions with organizations such as the Los Angeles Chargers, Earth Friendly Products (ECOS), and the Philadelphia Phillies. Through these opportunities, she has gained experience in event operations, customer service, marketing, and social media management. In addition, Katerina spent a semester studying abroad at the University of Otago in New Zealand, where she had the opportunity to experience international perspectives in sport and culture. Most recently, she joined the Philadelphia Phillies as a Ticket Services Representative, where she works directly with fans and contributes to game-day operations. These experiences have helped her develop strong communication, teamwork, and leadership skills that will support her career aspirations of working in the sports and events industry.

name: Abbi Komrofske
title: What Wins the Stanley Cup? Predicting Championship Probability Using NHL Playoff Performance Data
bio: Abbi Komrofske is a senior at Arcadia University pursuing a degree in Sport Management with a minor in Data Analytics. Abbi is a four-year member of Arcadia's Women's Ice Hockey team and has been playing hockey for over 15 years. Throughout her time as a collegiate athlete, she has developed strong leadership, teamwork, and time-management skills while balancing the demands of academics and athletics. During her time at Arcadia, Abbi has focused on combining her passion for sports with data and analytics to better understand performance and decision-making in athletics. Her academic work has emphasized statistical analysis and research in sports, particularly within hockey analytics. In recognition of her academic achievements, she received the Sport Management Distinguished Honor Award. These experiences have helped her develop strong analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills that will support her future career goals in the sports industry.

name: Jonathan Matthews
title: Explaining Goal Difference in the EPL: Home Advantage vs. Match Performance
bio: Jonathan Matthews is a senior at Arcadia University, majoring in Sport Management. He is also a member of Arcadia's Men's Soccer team. Over the course of Jonathan's college career, he has prepared his future in club and college soccer completing multiple coaching internships. At the end of his junior year, and furthering threw his senior year, he furthered his knowledge by interning with a local soccer club where he learned about the responsibilities of being a coach and bettering a team on his own. These internship opportunities have not only deepened his understanding of tactics and ways to improve players while having fun, but also elevated his interpersonal and interactional skills, preparing him for this career that awaits him. Jonathan has gained a lot more confidence than he had in the beginning of his internship.

name: Riley O'Hagan
title: Home-Court Advantage: An examination of whether game location affects winning outcomes in the WNBA
bio: Riley O'Hagan is a senior at Arcadia University pursuing a degree in Sport Management. Riley is a 3-year member of Arcadia's Women's Basketball team and has gained valuable experience through several internships throughout her college career. She has worked as a Sports Information Director (SID) intern at Arcadia University, where she assisted with athletic communications, media relations, and game day operations. Riley also completed an internship with the New York Mets, gaining experience within a professional sports organization. These opportunities have helped her develop a strong work ethic along with communication, media, and organizational skills that will support her career aspirations of working in athletic communications within professional sports.

name: Christopher Stanaway
title: Player Rank, Win Shares, and Value Over Replacement as Predictors of NBA Career Longevity
bio: Christopher Stanaway is a senior at Arcadia University pursuing a degree in Sport Management.

name: Chris Williams
title: NBA Player Salaries: Does age, statistics, or position affect a player's potential salary?
bio: Chris Williams is a senior at Arcadia University, pursuing a degree in Sport Management. Chris was a 4-year member of the Men's Basketball Team and has done various internships throughout those years. Following his sophomore year, he was an intern at Holy Family University, involved in an assistant athletic director role, helping to coordinate end of year events and game days. During his junior year, he was a player development and facilities intern for Full Court Philly AAU. In Chris's senior year, he became a sales intern for MADE Hoops AAU. These experiences helped him develop better communication, leadership, and management skills. Chris hopes to continue his work in helping student-athletes at all levels.
